comment_text: The new MCA21 corporate governance framework is excellent. It will greatly improve transparency and accountability for all stakeholders.
This regulation imposes unreasonable compliance burden on small businesses. The penalties are too harsh and implementation timeline is unrealistic.
The Ministry has released draft amendments covering board composition, audit requirements, and disclosure norms for public review.
I strongly support the digital transformation initiative. Online filing and e-governance will save time and reduce corruption significantly.
The proposed changes to related party transaction rules are confusing and will create legal uncertainty for companies operating across multiple sectors.
Environmental sustainability reporting requirements are a welcome addition. Companies must be held accountable for their carbon footprint.
Quarterly audit requirements are excessive for micro enterprises. A simplified annual compliance framework would be more appropriate.
The shareholder rights protection clauses are well drafted and will prevent minority shareholder exploitation by promoters.
Implementation of these regulations without adequate training and infrastructure will lead to widespread non-compliance and litigation.
The consultation process has been transparent and inclusive. The ministry has genuinely considered stakeholder feedback in drafting these amendments.
